name: Renderer Duration Field Support

tasks.title: Add DurationMS field to Record struct
tasks.description: Add DurationMS field to Record struct
tasks.body: In internal/logrender/record.go, add a DurationMS *int64 field to the Record struct with json tag duration_ms. Use a pointer so zero-value (absent field) is distinguishable from an explicit 0. Place the field near the other timing-related fields (after ExitCode is a natural spot).
acceptance_criteria: Record struct has a DurationMS pointer-to-int64 field with json duration_ms tag
ParseRecord correctly populates DurationMS from JSON input containing duration_ms

tasks.title: Update renderers to prefer pre-computed duration
tasks.description: Update renderers to prefer pre-computed duration
tasks.body: In internal/logrender/summary.go and internal/logrender/interleaved.go, modify the stage_complete and planning_complete handlers: if the record DurationMS is non-nil, convert it to time.Duration and use that instead of computing r.Timestamp.Sub(startTime). If DurationMS is nil, fall back to the existing timestamp-diff logic. This ensures backward compatibility with older log files that lack the field. Apply the same pattern in both Replay and Follow code paths.
acceptance_criteria: Renderers use DurationMS when present in the record
Renderers fall back to timestamp diff when DurationMS is nil
Both Replay and Follow code paths are updated

tasks.title: Add renderer tests for duration_ms consumption
tasks.description: Add renderer tests for duration_ms consumption
tasks.body: Write or extend tests for the summary and interleaved renderers to verify: (1) when a stage_complete record contains duration_ms, the rendered duration matches the pre-computed value (not the timestamp diff), (2) when a stage_complete record lacks duration_ms, the rendered duration is computed from timestamps as before, (3) ParseRecord correctly round-trips a duration_ms field. Use the existing test patterns in internal/logrender/.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies pre-computed duration_ms is used when present
Test verifies timestamp fallback when duration_ms is absent

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Renderer Duration Field Support is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify backward compatibility: renderers must still produce correct output from old log files without duration_ms

breadcrumbs.text: Added DurationMS *int64 field to Record struct with json:duration_ms tag; added tests for present, zero, and absent cases

task-0001.objective: Add a DurationMS *int64 field to the Record struct for duration_ms JSON deserialization
task-0001.what_happened: Added DurationMS *int64 with json:"duration_ms" tag to Record in internal/logrender/record.go, placed after ExitCode. Added three test cases in record_test.go covering present, zero-value, and absent duration_ms.
went_well: Pointer semantics naturally distinguish absent from zero, and ParseRecord's two-pass unmarshal handles the new field with no code changes needed
improvements: None; this was a minimal struct addition
action_items: Downstream tasks should wire DurationMS into renderers that display timing information
